# trovex

> trovex is the canonical doc store for AI coding agents. It indexes a repo's
> markdown and serves an agent the single current doc that answers a query — a
> path:line pointer with a freshness marker — instead of letting it reread the
> repo to guess which file is canonical. Same answers, about 60% fewer tokens
> per doc lookup. Open source, local-first (SQLite + ONNX), runs on your
> machine. Ships as an MCP server and a CLI.

## What it does
- Serves one canonical answer per query (with freshness: canonical / stale / duplicate), not a pile of candidate chunks to sift.
- Section-level reads: hands back the paragraph that answers, not the whole file, so a short answer costs short-answer tokens.
- Shared write path: one agent records what it learns once; every other agent and your teammates read it back — single source of truth by construction.
- Local-first: indexing and embeddings run on your machine in SQLite + ONNX. No cloud, no API keys, your code never leaves your machine.

## Who it's for
- Solo developers and small teams (1–20) running AI coding agents on real, doc-heavy repos.
- Anyone whose agents reread the same .md files every session and burn tokens guessing which doc is current.

## Comparisons
- [trovex vs CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md](https://trovex.dev/vs/claude-md/): a static instructions file vs one canonical doc routed per query.
- [trovex vs repomix / files-to-prompt](https://trovex.dev/vs/repomix/): packing the whole repo into a prompt vs retrieving the one answer.
- [trovex vs context-hub (CTX)](https://trovex.dev/vs/context-hub/): a ranked list of candidate chunks vs one canonical doc with a freshness marker.
- [All comparisons](https://trovex.dev/vs/)

## Answers
- [How do I stop my coding agent rereading the same files every session?](https://trovex.dev/answers/stop-agent-rereading-docs/): serve one canonical answer per query instead of raw files to sift.
- [How do I reduce the token cost of giving a coding agent context?](https://trovex.dev/answers/reduce-agent-token-costs/): cut the repeated retrieval, not the one-time prompt.
- [What is canonical context for coding agents?](https://trovex.dev/answers/canonical-context-for-agents/): the single current, authoritative doc that answers a query.
- [Does a bigger context window make rereading docs cheaper?](https://trovex.dev/answers/bigger-context-window-rereading/): no — it raises the ceiling, not the efficiency.
- [How do I keep multiple agents working from the same docs?](https://trovex.dev/answers/shared-source-of-truth-multiple-agents/): one shared read-and-write point, no drift.
- [Is there an MCP server that serves a repo's docs to coding agents?](https://trovex.dev/answers/mcp-server-for-repo-docs/): yes — trovex is an MCP server that returns the one canonical doc per query, not the whole repo.
- [What's the single source of truth for a multi-agent repo?](https://trovex.dev/answers/source-of-truth-multi-agent-repos/): one canonical, freshness-marked doc every agent reads and writes back, instead of each agent guessing from raw files.

## Deep dives
- [The token cost of coding agents rereading your docs](https://trovex.dev/blog/the-token-cost-of-agents-rereading-docs/): the full math — a typical doc lookup drops from ~720 to ~280 tokens (~60% fewer) when you serve one canonical doc instead of letting the agent reread the pile to guess which is current. Shows where the number comes from and how to reproduce it on your own repo.
- [MCP context patterns for coding agents: dump, retrieve, or answer](https://trovex.dev/blog/mcp-context-patterns-for-coding-agents/): the three ways to feed an agent project context over MCP (dump the repo, return candidate chunks, or serve one canonical answer), what each costs, and how to choose.
- [One source of truth for a fleet of coding agents](https://trovex.dev/blog/one-source-of-truth-for-a-fleet-of-agents/): once a second agent or teammate touches the repo, scattered notes re-derive and drift; a shared read/write path makes a thing learned once canonical for everyone.

## Setup (per MCP client)
- [trovex for Claude Code](https://trovex.dev/for/claude-code/): `claude mcp add --transport http trovex http://localhost:8765/mcp`.
- [trovex for Cursor](https://trovex.dev/for/cursor/): add it to `.cursor/mcp.json`, enable in Settings → MCP.
- [trovex for Windsurf](https://trovex.dev/for/windsurf/): `serverUrl` in `~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json`.
- [trovex for Cline](https://trovex.dev/for/cline/): a remote streamable-HTTP server in `cline_mcp_settings.json`.
- [All clients](https://trovex.dev/for/)

## Access
- Status: private beta. The repository is currently private; access is by invitation.
- Request access: https://trovex.dev/#waitlist (the install commands below work once you're in the beta).

## Links
- [Request beta access](https://trovex.dev/#waitlist): the private-beta waitlist.
- [Website](https://trovex.dev): the landing page.
- [Repository](https://github.com/Synergix-lab/trovex): source (private during the beta; core AGPL-3.0, CLIs MIT).
- [llms-full.txt](https://trovex.dev/llms-full.txt): the full extractable content in one file.

## Facts
- Category: context / memory infrastructure for AI coding agents.
- Interface: Model Context Protocol (MCP) server (local, streamable-HTTP at http://localhost:8765/mcp) + CLI.
- Works with: Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Zed, and any MCP client.
- License: core AGPL-3.0, CLIs MIT.
- Access: private beta — request at https://trovex.dev/#waitlist.
- Install (once in the beta): `uv run trovex index /path/to/repo` then `uv run trovex serve`.
